title: Freeze the Phase 3 triage workspace state model and route-command contract pack
risk: Phase 3 workspace implementation could reopen shell continuity, writability, or lease semantics through ad hoc local state.
impact: Staff review flows could diverge from the approved patient/support baseline and lose the same-shell trust and settlement discipline already frozen.
whyNotExitBlocker: This exit gate approves the current patient/support baseline only. Queue-task workspace contracts are explicitly the next publication step rather than part of the already-approved route family.

title: Freeze queue ranking, fairness, duplicate-cluster, and more-info review contracts
risk: Queue ordinals, duplicate handling, and more-info review mechanics could drift into unofficial formulas or hidden local merge logic.
impact: Later staff review flows could lose deterministic ordering and continuity guarantees even though the patient/support baseline remains sound.
whyNotExitBlocker: The current baseline does not expose Phase 3 queue ranking or duplicate review as live human-checkpoint routes. Those capabilities extend the approved baseline; they do not define it.

title: Freeze endpoint decision, approval, escalation, callback, and admin-resolution boundaries
risk: Endpoint, approval, escalation, and callback-resolution actions could fork from the current patient/support continuity and settlement language once writable triage flows land.
impact: Patient reply, callback, and support settlement semantics could fragment across the later human-checkpoint workflow unless the downstream action contracts stay bounded.
whyNotExitBlocker: Those mutable human-checkpoint decisions are downstream of the already-approved patient/support baseline. They must consume this baseline rather than hold it open.

title: Replay the baseline against credentialled live environments and provider bindings
risk: Credentialled NHS login, live webhook behavior, or live provider retries could drift from the simulator-backed assumptions that underpin the repository-runnable baseline.
impact: Portal recovery, callback repair, support replay, and controlled resend could be over-read as production-proven when only repository-runnable proof exists.
whyNotExitBlocker: The 225 verdict explicitly approves the repository-runnable baseline, not live-provider operation. Live replay remains outside the current gate by design.

title: Obtain production clinical-safety, DSPT, rollback, and operational signoff
risk: Release consumers could over-read the baseline exit as clinical-safety, DSPT, or operational deployment approval.
impact: Production promotion could occur without the separate hazard, privacy, rollback, and runtime-assurance gates that must remain explicit.
whyNotExitBlocker: This gate is a product-baseline gate for the patient/support family. Production release approval remains a distinct governance boundary and is intentionally not claimed here.
